title: Geometry of Dyck Paths
speaker: Syu Kato
abstract: There are two major research trends in the theory of symmetric functions arising from Dyck paths. One is the theory of Catalan symmetric functions and its geometric realization conceived by Chen-Haiman, following the works of Broer and Shimozono-Weyman. The symmetric function part of this story was established by a series of works of Blasiak-Morse-Pun-Summers. Another is the study of chromatic symmetric functions of graphs, that utilize the fact that Dyck paths roughly correspond to unit interval graphs. We first exhibit a family of smooth algebraic varieties that realize Catalan symmetric functions, and explain how it imply the geometric predictions of Broer, Shimozono-Weyman, Chen-Haiman, and Blasiak-Morse-Pun. We then exhibit that our varieties also represents the chromatic symmetric functions of unit interval graphs. Thus, our varieties might be understood as a geometric realization of the world of Dyck paths (which are related but not the same as the Hessenberg varieties). If time and situation allows, we will present some more recent works on this subject. This talk is based on arXiv:2301.00862 and arXiv:2410.12231.
tags: Workshop on Combinatorics of Enumerative Geometry

proof_stages.label: Dyck paths as B-stable nilpotent subspaces
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines Dyck paths and associates to each path psi a B-stable subspace n_psi of the nilpotent radical of gl_n by prescribing which upper-triangular matrix entries are allowed.

proof_stages.label: Classical cotangent-bundle Kostka theorem
proof_stages.summary: For X=G/B and T^*X isomorphic to G times^B n, Borel-Weil line bundles and their pullbacks have graded global sections whose G-multiplicities are Kostka-Foulkes polynomials, with higher cohomology vanishing.

proof_stages.label: Dyck-path Kostka conjectures
proof_stages.summary: The classical theorem is proposed in a Dyck-path version for G times^B n_psi, defining psi-Kostka polynomials and asking for positivity and higher-cohomology vanishing beyond known Euler-characteristic results.

proof_stages.label: Affine-Grassmannian compactification
proof_stages.summary: Loop-group and affine-Grassmannian geometry, via an affine type-A Bott-Samelson or iterated projective-bundle construction, provides a smooth projective ambient space in which the Dyck-path space is compactified as X_psi.

proof_stages.label: Cohomology theorem and boundary limit
proof_stages.summary: The compactifications X_psi are smooth projective and carry line bundles whose global sections recover psi-Kostka multiplicities for polynomial dominant weights, with higher cohomology vanishing and a boundary-divisor limit yielding the desired open-space statement.

proof_stages.label: Unit-interval graphs and q-chromatic symmetric functions
proof_stages.summary: The complement pattern of n_psi defines a unit interval graph Gamma_psi, whose q-chromatic symmetric function is defined by proper colorings and ascents and is tied to Hessenberg cohomology and e-positivity questions.

proof_stages.label: Sheaf-theoretic realization in the affine Grassmannian
proof_stages.summary: The map X_psi to the affine Grassmannian is pushed forward, and the decomposition theorem expresses it in IC summands whose graded multiplicity spaces encode the Schur coefficients of the chromatic symmetric function.

proof_stages.label: Localization formula and affine-Weyl positivity conjecture
proof_stages.summary: Localization of X_psi yields explicit affine symmetric-group or nil-Hecke elements S_psi giving the q=1 chromatic symmetric function, and this motivates a broader positivity conjecture for smooth maps into the affine Grassmannian.
